title: rumex angiocarpus var. australis
intro: Rumex angiocarpus var. australis, also known as the Australian dock or sea dock, is a variety of the Rumex angiocarpus plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high levels of anthraquinones, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Rumex angiocarpus var. australis include its potential to treat digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural laxative. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves or roots in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
